seoTitle: How to Add Users to a Cloudflare Account (2026)
metaDescription: Invite team members to your Cloudflare account, scope their access to specific domains, and assign role-based permissions.
intro: As your team grows, you'll likely want developers or an agency to have their own access to Cloudflare without sharing a single login. This guide covers inviting new users, scoping their domain access, and assigning appropriate roles.

steps.heading: Confirm you have Super Administrator rights
steps.body: Make sure your own account has the permission level needed to invite new members before starting.

steps.heading: Open Manage Account
steps.body: Scroll to the bottom left sidebar and select the Manage Account directory, then open the Members tab.

steps.heading: Click Invite
steps.body: Click the Invite button to open the new user configuration panel.

steps.heading: Set the email and access scope
steps.body: Enter the collaborator's email address and configure whether they can see all domains or only specific production or staging zones.

steps.heading: Assign roles and send
steps.body: Choose an appropriate role, such as developer or marketing access, review the summary, and click Send Invite. Track pending invitations and review the audit log afterward.

faq.question: Can I limit a new user to only one domain?
faq.answer: Yes, when inviting a member you can configure access scopes to include or exclude specific domains in your account.

faq.question: What permissions do I need to invite team members?
faq.answer: You generally need Super Administrator rights on the account to send new user invitations.

faq.question: How do I check if an invitation is still pending?
faq.answer: The Members tab shows the status of sent invitations, letting you track which ones haven't been accepted yet.

faq.question: Can I remove a user's access later?
faq.answer: Yes, administrators can edit or revoke a member's role and access scope at any time from the same Members management panel.
